events.text: Patient is a 62-year-old male of White/Caucasian race, non-Hispanic ethnicity, with a BMI of 27.3 and a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and hypertension.

At age 62, patient presented with painless jaundice, dark urine, and a 15-pound unintentional weight loss over two months; CT pancreas protocol revealed a 3.2 cm mass in the head of the pancreas (C25.0) with abutment of the superior mesenteric vein without occlusion, classified as borderline resectable.

At age 62, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol showed a 3.2 cm hypodense mass in the pancreatic head with abutment of the SMV over approximately 180 degrees without vessel occlusion or deformity; no distant metastatic disease; mild intrahepatic and extrahepatic biliary ductal dilation noted.

At age 62, ERCP performed for biliary obstruction secondary to pancreatic head mass; a 10 mm x 60 mm fully covered metal biliary stent was placed across the distal common bile duct stricture with good bile drainage achieved.

At age 62, patient experienced grade 2 biliary obstruction with total bilirubin of 8.4 mg/dL and direct bilirubin of 6.1 mg/dL, which resolved to near-normal levels following ERCP with stent placement.

At age 62, EUS with FNA of the pancreatic head mass was performed; the mass measured 3.4 x 2.8 cm by endoscopic ultrasound with portal vein abutment; multiple passes obtained for cytology and cell block.

At age 62, EUS-guided FNA of pancreatic head mass: cytology demonstrates clusters of atypical glandular cells with nuclear enlargement, irregular nuclear membranes, and prominent nucleoli, consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology code 8140/3); immunostains positive for CK7, CK19, and MUC1; final diagnosis: pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.

At age 62, next-generation sequencing panel on the FNA specimen revealed KRAS G12D mutation (variant allele frequency 34%), TP53 R175H mutation, CDKN2A loss, BRCA1/2 wild-type, PALB2 wild-type, microsatellite stable (MSS), tumor mutational burden 4 mutations/Mb.

At age 62, CA 19-9 tumor marker at diagnosis was 842 U/mL (reference range less than 37 U/mL); CEA was 6.2 ng/mL; staging determined as cT3N0M0, Stage IIA, borderline resectable due to SMV abutment; multidisciplinary tumor board recommended neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX.

At age 62, patient started on pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (CREON 36,000 units with meals) for pancreatic exocrine insufficiency with steatorrhea; nutritional counseling initiated with high-calorie, high-protein diet plan.

At age 62, patient began neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2 46-hour infusion, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days, cycle 1 of planned 8 cycles.
At age 62, after cycle 2 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia with ANC of 0.8 x 10^9/L; cycle 3 was delayed by one week and irinotecan dose was reduced by 20%.
At age 62, after cycle 4 of FOLFIRINOX, patient reported grade 2 diarrhea with 5-6 loose stools per day and grade 1 nausea managed with ondansetron and loperamide.
At age 62, mid-treatment CA 19-9 decreased from 842 to 214 U/mL after 4 cycles of FOLFIRINOX, indicating biochemical response.
At age 62, patient completed 8 cycles of neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX with dose modifications; cumulative grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in hands and feet noted.

At age 62, restaging CT pancreas protocol after 8 cycles of neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX showed interval decrease in the pancreatic head mass from 3.2 cm to 1.8 cm; SMV abutment decreased with improved fat plane; no new hepatic lesions or peritoneal implants; CA 19-9 decreased to 87 U/mL; findings consistent with partial response and now resectable disease.
At age 62, PET/CT showed FDG-avid mass in pancreatic head with SUVmax of 4.2 (decreased from 8.7 at diagnosis); no FDG-avid distant metastatic disease.

At age 62, patient underwent a pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with portal vein resection and reconstruction; operative findings included a firm mass in the pancreatic head with adherence to the portal vein requiring segmental resection; estimated blood loss 650 mL; no intraoperative complications.

At age 62, surgical pathology from Whipple specimen: residual moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, 1.4 cm in greatest dimension; extensive treatment effect with 60% tumor necrosis and fibrosis (treatment response grade 2); margins: R0 resection, all margins negative with closest margin 3 mm at the uncinate; 2 of 18 lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma; pathologic staging ypT2N1M0; lymphovascular invasion present, perineural invasion present.

At age 63, post-operative recovery complicated by grade 2 delayed gastric emptying requiring prolonged nasogastric decompression; resolved by post-operative day 14; pancreatic fistula grade A managed conservatively.
At age 63, patient developed grade 2 pancreatic exocrine insufficiency post-Whipple requiring increased pancreatic enzyme supplementation; weight stabilized after dietary optimization.
At age 63, patient began adjuvant gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles combined with capecitabine 1660 mg/m2/day on days 1-21 of 28-day cycles, for planned 6 cycles.
At age 63, during cycle 3 of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine, patient developed grade 2 hand-foot syndrome with painful erythema and desquamation of palms; capecitabine dose reduced by 25%.
At age 63, surveillance CA 19-9 after completion of adjuvant therapy was 22 U/mL within normal range; CT abdomen/pelvis showed no evidence of recurrence.
At age 63, patient completed 6 cycles of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine with dose modifications; transitioned to surveillance.
At age 63, surveillance CT at 3 months post-adjuvant therapy showed no evidence of local recurrence or distant metastatic disease; post-surgical changes stable.
At age 63, patient managing well on pancreatic enzyme replacement; weight stable at 168 lbs; pain well-controlled without narcotics; continued diabetes management with metformin and insulin.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 62, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol showed a 3.2 cm hypodense mass in the pancreatic head with abutment of the SMV over approximately 180 degrees without vessel occlusion or deformity; no distant metastatic disease; mild intrahepatic and extrahepatic biliary ductal dilation noted.

Please correlate clinically.

At age 62, ERCP performed for biliary obstruction secondary to pancreatic head mass; a 10 mm x 60 mm fully covered metal biliary stent was placed across the distal common bile duct stricture with good bile drainage achieved.

scenario_blurb: Locally advanced or borderline resectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Treated with neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX, some achieving resectability and undergoing Whipple procedure, some with disease progression. Include CA 19-9 monitoring, CT imaging response assessment.
